bullets.text: Q1 2023 companywide CR was 99.0, missing the 96 target; March alone posted a 106.2 CR driven by unfavorable prior-year reserve development of 4.6 points, catastrophe losses of 1.8 points, and ongoing elevated loss severity.

Loss severity rose nearly 10% year over year in Q1 2023, similar to Q4 2022; vehicle repair costs (property damage, collision, comprehensive) drove most of the 4.6-point unfavorable prior accident year reserve development, with Florida tort changes adding less than 1 point.

Personal auto rates increased over 13% in 2022 and an additional 4% in Q1 2023; management planned to be aggressive with further rate increases through the remainder of 2023 in response to continued inflationary pressure.
Companywide NPW grew a strong 22% in Q1 2023 with Personal Lines up 25% and Commercial Lines up 15%; growth reflected rate increases earning in and strong new business application volume.

Companywide PIF grew 9% year over year in Q1 2023, with Personal Lines up 10%; new application growth reflected elevated consumer shopping and improved conversion alongside better-than-expected renewal retention.

Debt-to-total capital ratio fell from 28.7% at year-end 2022 to 27.5% at Q1 2023; investment portfolio returned 2.3% for the quarter with fixed-income at 2.0% and equities at 7.3%; management maintained a conservative, more defensive posture.

24 catastrophic weather events in Q1 2023 versus 11 in Q1 2022 contributed 1.8 points to the companywide CR; March storms drove the majority of catastrophe losses, with Property bearing elevated wind, hail, and tornado activity.

Advertising spend in Q1 2023 was 23% above Q1 2022, consistent with peak shopping seasonality; management flagged it would reduce media investment if profitability did not recover, using ad spend as a key lever to manage the combined ratio.
